author: msjsc001
description: Render and navigate Logseq-style UUID block references and block embeds in Obsidian, with outlines, backlinks, and page-property/YAML sync. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: msjsc001
manifest.description: Render and navigate UUID-based block references and block embeds in Live Preview and Reading Mode, with Logseq-style outlines, property hiding, source badges, backlinks, and local indexing.
manifest.name: Block Reference Enhancer

name: Block Reference Enhancer

releases.author: msjsc001
releases.description:
## Added

- Added a General settings section.
- Added a default-enabled option to show or hide block index status and file, block, and reference counts in the bottom-right status bar.

## Improved

- Renamed the Experimental outline section to Outline enhancements.
- Updated English and Chinese documentation for the new settings organization.

## Fixed

- Fixed the block index status remaining visible after its display setting was disabled.
- Hiding the status display does not stop background indexing.

releases.author: msjsc001
releases.description:
## Performance

- Reduced CPU and layout pressure on pages containing many dense block references.
- Reused horizontal layout measurements instead of recalculating every rendered reference after vertical reflow.
- Continued batched inline rendering through a bounded queue without rescanning the full page for every batch.
- Cancelled outdated render queues after editing, navigation, selection, index, or settings changes.
- Preserved correct measurement refreshes when themes, fonts, list indentation, or editor width change.

## Documentation

- Updated the English and Chinese documentation with the new dense-page rendering behavior.

releases.author: msjsc001
releases.description:
## Fixed

- Kept block reference and block embed indentation stable in the Minimal theme by measuring the actual editor line boundary.
- Synchronized block embed fold states when switching between Live Preview and Reading Mode.

## Improved

- Added Minimal as an actively maintained and recommended theme alongside the Obsidian default theme.
- Added regression coverage for inline widget geometry across different editor layouts.

releases.author: msjsc001
releases.description:
## Added

- Added automatic interface localization for English and Simplified Chinese.
- Chinese Obsidian locales, including `zh-TW`, now use the Simplified Chinese interface.
- Unsupported interface languages automatically fall back to English.
- Localized commands, settings, context menus, dialogs, notices, index status messages, autocomplete actions, reference popovers, outline paste, and page-property synchronization.

## Improved

- Added localization completeness tests to prevent missing translations or mismatched placeholders.
- Updated English and Chinese documentation with interface language behavior.

releases.author: msjsc001
releases.description:
## Added

- Added experimental whitelist-based synchronization between Logseq page properties and Obsidian YAML frontmatter.
- Added manual synchronization for the current file and selected folders.
- Added conflict resolution, cancellable batch processing, progress notices, and detailed reports.
- Added strict repair for YAML frontmatter that Logseq has converted into an unordered-list structure.
- Added a safe option to remove fully represented YAML and return selected folders to Logseq-only page properties.

## Safety

- The feature is disabled by default.
- Only whitelisted properties are synchronized.
- Unknown properties and unsupported complex YAML values are preserved and skipped.
- Files changed after batch scanning are not overwritten.

releases.author: msjsc001
releases.description:
## Improved

- Added a compact collapse control to the source-reference popover.
- Collapsed reference lists now show only the reference type and file name.
- UUID block-reference syntax in preview contexts is resolved into readable source-block summaries.
- Removed redundant UUID metadata and full file paths to provide more space for useful reference context.
- Added cycle, missing-block, and recursion-depth protection for nested UUID preview resolution.

releases.author: msjsc001
releases.description:
## Improved

- Show complete UUID block reference text without ellipsis truncation.
- Add persistent outline folding for block embeds in Live Preview and Reading Mode.
- Add compact circular fold controls for embed roots and nested list items.
- Replace duplicate list bullets with fold controls where applicable.
- Apply Logseq-style property hiding consistently inside rendered block embeds.

## Fixed

- Refresh block embeds when property-hiding settings change.
- Remove obsolete fold state when an embed occurrence is deleted or changed.
- Preserve current source content updates while retaining per-embed fold state.

releases.author: msjsc001
releases.description:
## Improved

- Removed ordinary blank separator lines from `Paste clipboard as outline`, keeping generated unordered-list outlines continuous.
- Normalized every generated child level to exactly one Tab followed by `- `.
- Preserved required blank lines and relative indentation inside fenced code blocks.
- Added `Process` and `Cancel` confirmation for large clipboard content.
- Large outline conversions now run in short time slices with visible progress and bounded safety limits.
- Captured the original file and outline location before processing, so switching pages no longer redirects the final insertion.
- Added safe target relocation when the original file changes during processing.
- Cancelled insertion when the original target is deleted or cannot be resolved uniquely.
- Prevented concurrent outline-paste jobs and partial document insertion.

## Fixed

- Fixed large HTML clipboard structures being rejected by the normal-size parser limit.
- Fixed outline insertion using stale editor offsets after asynchronous processing.
- Fixed inconsistent indentation caused by mixed spaces and Tabs in clipboard content.

releases.author: msjsc001
releases.description:
## Added

- Added `Copy selection (UUID blocks as text)` to the editor context menu.
- Converts selected `((uuid))` references into their visible summaries.
- Converts standalone `{{embed ((uuid))}}` embeds into readable Markdown outlines with child blocks.
- Preserves ordinary selected Markdown and keeps exactly one root list marker for expanded embeds.

## Safety

- Normal `Ctrl/Cmd + C` behavior is unchanged.
- Incomplete UUID syntax and UUID-like text inside inline or fenced code are not converted.
- Missing blocks, cyclic embeds, recursion depth, and output size are handled safely.

releases.author: msjsc001
releases.description:
## Fixed

- Removed duplicate native browser tooltips from the Back, Delete, and source reference badge controls.
- Kept the animated Obsidian-style tooltips and accessibility labels.
- Improved TypeScript standard-library compatibility for community review by using ES2020 type declarations.

## Notes

- No block reference, block embed, index, cache, or navigation behavior was changed.
- Version bumped to 1.3.13.

releases.author: msjsc001
releases.description:
## Fixed

- moved invisible source-anchor widget styling from inline assignments to CSS classes, fixing the community review blocker
- switched editor context menu targeting and outline paste DOM checks to cross-window-safe helpers
- replaced the outline paste scheduler's bare timeout usage with `window.setTimeout`
- removed the deprecated rectangle typing in widget coordinate helpers

## Notes

- version bumped to 1.3.12
- no user-facing block reference, embed, index, or interaction behavior was changed in this patch

releases.author: msjsc001
releases.description:
## 1.3.11

### Fixed
- Improved stability for dense pages with many rendered block references and embeds
- Reduced visible churn during scrolling, tab switching, and rapid focus changes in Live Preview
- Improved inline block-reference recovery so rendered references return sooner after the cursor leaves
- Improved width handling for long inline references to reduce wrap jumps in deeply nested outlines

### Improved
- Added a dedicated internal coordinate anchor for better compatibility with third-party editor measurement
- Updated English and Simplified Chinese documentation for the latest rendering stability behavior

### Notes
- Community release assets should include `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css`
- The Obsidian default theme remains the baseline theme officially expected to work correctly

releases.author: msjsc001
releases.description:
## Fixed

- Fixed a Live Preview issue where inline block references could stay in raw `((uuid))` source form too long after the cursor moved away.
- Inline block references now return to their rendered state automatically shortly after the cursor no longer touches them.

## Documentation

- Updated English and Chinese documentation to describe the restored inline reference behavior in Live Preview.

releases.author: msjsc001
releases.description:
## Improved

- Improved the experimental outline paste workflow for unordered-list blocks
- Added more reliable right-click target detection for outline actions
- Improved support for empty unordered-list items in outline actions
- Improved hierarchy recovery for structured web and ChatGPT content
- Kept generated child outline lists using Tab indentation
- Added `Copy current level and children` for copying the current outline subtree as raw Markdown

## Docs

- Updated English and Chinese documentation for the experimental outline paste feature
- Clarified support boundaries for empty list items and structured web content

## Validation

- `npm test`
- `npm run build`

releases.author: msjsc001
releases.description:
## Fixed

- Fixed compatibility for outline blocks that contain fenced code blocks inside list structures
- Fixed cases where some notes would not render block references, block embeds, or hidden property lines until the plugin cache was fully rebuilt
- Automatically invalidate outdated local block-index caches and trigger a full rebuild after parser upgrades
- Improved source badge behavior and reduced duplicate badge rendering in rendered views

## Improved

- Added `Back` and `Delete` action buttons for rendered block references and block embeds
- Improved rendered-reference interaction behavior in Live Preview
- Updated English and Chinese documentation
- Clarified that the Obsidian default theme is the compatibility baseline for this plugin, and other themes are not guaranteed to render or behave correctly

## Notes

- Version updated to `1.3.8`
- Release assets: `main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css`

releases.author: msjsc001
releases.description:
## 1.3.7

### Fixed
- Fixed Logseq-style fenced code block compatibility inside list items
- Restored block reference rendering, block embed rendering, and property hiding for affected pages that were previously skipped after fenced code sections
- Added automatic cache invalidation and full rebuild when older parser results are no longer compatible

### Improved
- The plugin now detects outdated local index caches automatically after parser upgrades
- Users no longer need to manually delete `data.json`, remove the plugin folder, or run `Rebuild block reference index` first after this parser compatibility upgrade

### Notes
- This release performs a one-time full rebuild automatically when an older incompatible cache is detected
- After the rebuild completes, later startups return to normal cached loading and incremental updates

releases.author: msjsc001
releases.description:
## Fixed

- Make `Enter` create a first child block when hidden Logseq-style property lines are enabled
- Keep hidden property lines, continuation text, fenced blocks, and existing child trees attached to the parent block
- Support splitting list-item body text at the cursor so the right side becomes the new child block content

## Changed

- Restore `Hide Logseq-style property lines` to on by default for new installs while preserving existing saved settings
- Update Chinese and English documentation
- Expand the recommended companion plugin section
- Refresh the roadmap and remove the completed right-click source creation item

releases.author: msjsc001
releases.description:
## Fixed

- Stabilized hidden Logseq-style property lines during editing so they no longer flash back into view while typing.
- Stabilized source block reference-count badges during editing so they stay aligned with their source blocks.
- Fixed inconsistent unordered-list marker rendering inside block embeds.

## Notes

- Version bumped to 1.3.4.
- No changes to block reference syntax, block embed syntax, existing settings, or stored note content.

releases.author: msjsc001
releases.description:
## Improved

- Added outline-aware context previews to the source reference badge popover.
- Reference and embed results now show the current list item plus available parent and child list context.
- Hidden Logseq-style property lines are excluded from popover context previews.

releases.author: msjsc001
releases.description:
## 1.3.2

### Fixed
- Rename the settings page heading to a neutral title so the community plugin review no longer flags the plugin name inside settings headings

### Notes
- This is a minimal review-compatibility release
- No plugin behavior, commands, index logic, cache logic, or rendering behavior changed

releases.author: msjsc001
releases.description:
## 1.3.1

### Fixed
- Refresh block references and embeds automatically after a source block is saved
- Prefer the most recently modified active source block when the same UUID exists in multiple source locations

### Improved
- Added help/support entry points and issue templates
- Updated Chinese and English documentation for saved-source synchronization behavior

releases.author: msjsc001
releases.description:
## Highlights
- Add Logseq-style property-line hiding for unordered-list blocks in Live Preview and Reading Mode.
- Add plugin settings for hidden property keys, including exact-key and prefix-key rules.
- Update Chinese and English documentation for property hiding behavior and default rules.

## Assets
- main.js
- manifest.json
- styles.css

releases.author: msjsc001
releases.description:
## Highlights
- Added editor context menu actions for the current outline block: Copy block reference and Copy block embed.
- The new context menu actions reuse the existing id:: uuid when present and only create a new UUID when the current block does not already have one.
- Updated the Chinese and English documentation to describe the new right-click workflow.
- Added handoff planning documents for the context menu workflow and the future block-search roadmap.
## Assets
- main.js
- manifest.json
- styles.css

releases.author: msjsc001
releases.description:
## Highlights
- Added Copy current block embed to copy {{embed ((uuid))}} for the current source block.
- Block copy commands now reuse an existing id:: uuid instead of generating a new UUID when the source block already has one.
- Added a hover-only Back button for rendered block references and embeds so they can jump back to their source blocks.
- When the same UUID has multiple active source blocks, the plugin now opens a selection menu before jumping.
- Updated the Chinese and English documentation for the new commands, source navigation, and current plugin behavior.
## Assets
- main.js
- manifest.json
- styles.css

releases.author: msjsc001
releases.description:
## Highlights
- Improved cross-window and popup compatibility in the editor and reference popover.
- Refined block rendering, source-reference badge, and related DOM utility behavior.
- Updated Chinese and English documentation for current plugin usage and release guidance.
- Localized plan documents and added a contributing guide.

## Assets
- main.js
- manifest.json
- styles.css

releases.author: msjsc001
releases.description:
Block Reference Enhancer community submission release.

Assets:
- main.js
- manifest.json
- styles.css
